title: The Winds Of Fate
content:
One ship drives east and another drives west,
    With the self-same winds that blow,
         'Tis the set of the sails
         And not the gales
    That tell them the way to go.
    Like the winds of the sea are the winds of fate,
    As we voyage along through life,
         'Tis the set of the soul
         That decides its goal
    And not the calm or the strife.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ella Wheeler Wilcox delivers a powerful performance in "The Winds Of Fate"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
